metadata.title: Guideline on Real Time Release Testing (formerly Guideline on Parametric Release)

rules.rule_text: Medicinal products must comply with their approved specifications before they are released into the market.
rules.source_section: Executive Summary
rules.verbatim_quote: Medicinal products must comply with their approved specifications before they are released into the market.
rules.exceptions: Under certain conditions, an alternative strategy to systematic end product testing is possible, including RTRT when pre-authorized.

rules.rule_text: RTRT requires pre-authorization by the competent authority before it may be introduced.
rules.source_section: Section 4 - General considerations
rules.verbatim_quote: The introduction of RTRT requires pre-authorization by the competent authority.

rules.rule_text: Documentation submitted for a new market authorization or a variation for RTRT should address only those quality aspects that are specific for the medicinal product; site-specific quality aspects not specific to the product fall within the remit of GMPs.
rules.source_section: Section 4 - General considerations
rules.verbatim_quote: In general the documentation submitted for a new market authorization or a variation should address only those quality aspects that are specific for the medicinal product. Site specific quality aspects not specific to the product (i.e. quality systems and process implementation), fall within the remit of GMPs.

rules.rule_text: Adequate risk management must be demonstrated in line with the relevant ICH guidelines when introducing RTRT, whether as part of a new application or following a variation of an existing marketing authorization.
rules.source_section: Section 4 - General considerations
rules.verbatim_quote: In both cases adequate risk management should be demonstrated in line with the relevant ICH guidelines.

rules.rule_text: Approval as well as withdrawal of RTR testing is at the discretion of the Competent Authority; a withdrawal may be based on the results of an inspection or on the receipt of other information.
rules.source_section: Section 4 - General considerations
rules.verbatim_quote: Approval as well as withdrawal of RTR testing is at the discretion of the Competent Authority. A withdrawal may be based on the results of an inspection or on the receipt of other information.

rules.rule_text: If a company after approval of RTRT wishes to return to end product testing, a variation of the marketing authorization is required.
rules.source_section: Section 4 - General considerations
rules.verbatim_quote: If a company after approval of RTRT wishes to return to end product testing, a variation of the marketing authorization is required.

rules.rule_text: A specification must be established and each batch of a product should comply with it if tested, even when RTRT has been approved and end product testing is not routinely performed.
rules.source_section: Section 5.1 - RTRT as part of a Control Strategy
rules.verbatim_quote: Nevertheless a specification has to be established and each batch of a product should comply with it if tested.

rules.rule_text: A specification is necessary for stability studies, in order to establish a product shelf-life as well as for Official Medicines Control Laboratory (OMCL) controls.
rules.source_section: Section 5.1 - RTRT as part of a Control Strategy
rules.verbatim_quote: A specification is also necessary for stability studies, in order to establish a product shelf-life as well as for Official Medicines Control Laboratory (OMCL) controls.

rules.rule_text: The application for RTRT must be supported by adequate validation of the RTR test method.
rules.source_section: Section 5.1 - RTRT as part of a Control Strategy
rules.verbatim_quote: The application for RTRT should be supported by adequate validation of the RTR test method.

rules.rule_text: The relationship between the RTR test (including acceptance criteria) and the end product test and associated specification must be well understood and, where applicable, supported by substantial comparative data at commercial scale (parallel testing).
rules.source_section: Section 5.1 - RTRT as part of a Control Strategy
rules.verbatim_quote: The relationship between the RTR test, including acceptance criteria, and the end product test and associated specification should be well understood and, where applicable, supported by substantial comparative data at commercial scale (parallel testing).

rules.rule_text: When RTRT has been approved it must be routinely used for batch release.
rules.source_section: Section 5.1 - RTRT as part of a Control Strategy
rules.verbatim_quote: When RTRT has been approved this should be routinely used for batch release.

rules.rule_text: In the event that the test results of RTRT fail or are trending toward failure, RTRT may not be substituted by end-product testing.
rules.source_section: Section 5.1 - RTRT as part of a Control Strategy
rules.verbatim_quote: In the event that the test results of RTRT fail or are trending toward failure, RTRT may not be substituted by end-product testing.

rules.rule_text: Any RTRT failure must be investigated and trending must be followed up appropriately; batch release decisions must be made based on the results of these investigations and must comply with the content of the marketing authorization and current GMP requirements.
rules.source_section: Section 5.1 - RTRT as part of a Control Strategy
rules.verbatim_quote: Any failure should be investigated and trending should be followed up appropriately. Batch release decisions will need to be made based on the results of these investigations, and must comply with the content of the marketing authorization and current GMP requirements.

rules.rule_text: Attributes (e.g. uniformity of content) that are indirectly controlled by approved RTRT must still appear in the Certificate of Analysis for batches.
rules.source_section: Section 5.1 - RTRT as part of a Control Strategy
rules.verbatim_quote: Attributes (e.g. uniformity of content) that is indirectly controlled by approved RTRT should still appear in the Certificate of Analysis for batches.

rules.rule_text: The approved method for end-product testing must be mentioned in the Certificate of Analysis with the results given as 'Complies if tested' with a footnote: 'Controlled by approved Real Time Release testing'.
rules.source_section: Section 5.1 - RTRT as part of a Control Strategy
rules.verbatim_quote: The approved method for end-product testing should be mentioned and the results given as "Complies if tested" with a footnote: "Controlled by approved Real Time Release testing".

rules.rule_text: The control strategy must include a contingency plan specifying the use of alternative testing or monitoring approaches on a temporary basis in case of equipment failure; testing or monitoring equipment breakdown must be managed in the context of a deviation under the Quality Management System.
rules.source_section: Section 5.1 - RTRT as part of a Control Strategy
rules.verbatim_quote: In case of equipment failure the control strategy provided in the application should include a contingency plan specifying the use of alternative testing or monitoring approaches on a temporary basis. In this situation, the alternative approach could involve use of end-product testing or other options, while maintaining an acceptable level of quality. Testing or monitoring equipment breakdown needs to be managed in the context of a deviation under the Quality Management System and can be covered by GMP.
rules.exceptions: Alternative approach could involve end-product testing on a temporary basis.

rules.rule_text: The RTRT strategy must be based on a firm understanding of the process and of the relationship between process parameters, in-process material attributes and product attributes.
rules.source_section: Section 5.2 - Application of RTRT
rules.verbatim_quote: Critically, the RTRT strategy should be based on a firm understanding of the process and of the relationship between process parameters, in-process material attributes and product attributes.

rules.rule_text: If testing of units is part of the RTRT, a sampling strategy must be defined that provides the number of locations sampled throughout the batch as well as the number of dosage units tested at each location.
rules.source_section: Section 5.2 - Application of RTRT
rules.verbatim_quote: If testing of units is part of the RTRT a sampling strategy should be defined that provides the number of locations sampled throughout the batch as well as the number of dosage units tested at each location.
rules.exceptions: Applies only when testing of units is part of the RTRT.

rules.rule_text: When RTRT is applied, the attribute that is indirectly controlled together with a reference to the associated test procedure must be included in the specification as 'Conforms if tested'.
rules.source_section: Section 5.2 - Application of RTRT
rules.verbatim_quote: When RTRT is applied, the attribute that is indirectly controlled (e.g. sterility, uniformity of content) together with a reference to the associated test procedure, should still be included in the specification as "Conforms if tested" (see 5.1).

rules.rule_text: The relationship between end-product testing, material attributes, process monitoring and acceptance criteria must be fully explained and justified; any prediction models must be fully explained, justified and verified at the commercial site.
rules.source_section: Section 5.2 - Application of RTRT
rules.verbatim_quote: The relationship between end-product testing, material attributes, process monitoring and acceptance criteria, should be fully explained and justified. In addition, the use of any prediction models should be fully explained, justified and verified at the commercial site.

rules.rule_text: For biological/biotechnological products, an alternate approach to routine end product testing may only be considered for quality attributes where it can be demonstrated that an acceptable level would be maintained in the final product level, if tested.
rules.source_section: Section 5.2.1 - Application of RTRT to biological/biotechnological products
rules.verbatim_quote: Alternate approach to routine end product testing could be considered for quality attributes included in the specification of a biological/biotechnological product, provided that it can be demonstrated that acceptable level would be maintained in the final product level, if tested.

rules.rule_text: For biological products, a routine testing approach for process-related impurities such as residual host cell DNA or host cell proteins must be based on monitoring, using suitable analytical tools, of the level of those impurities at appropriate step(s) of the process to ensure acceptable levels in the final product.
rules.source_section: Section 5.2.1 - Application of RTRT to biological/biotechnological products
rules.verbatim_quote: A routine testing approach would be based on the monitoring, using suitable analytical tools, of the level of those impurities at appropriate step(s) of the process, in order to ensure acceptable levels in the final product.
rules.exceptions: A validation approach may alternatively be used, based on evidence of successful validation of the manufacturing process.

rules.rule_text: A validation approach for biological product impurities must be based on evidence of successful validation of the manufacturing process establishing that at given steps of the purification scheme, impurities are removed in a consistent and reproducible manner to an acceptable level.
rules.source_section: Section 5.2.1 - Application of RTRT to biological/biotechnological products
rules.verbatim_quote: A validation approach would be based on evidence of successful validation of the manufacturing process establishing that at given steps of the purification scheme, those impurities are removed in a consistent and reproducible manner to an acceptable level.
rules.exceptions: A combination of routine testing and validation approaches is also possible.

rules.rule_text: For products coming from third countries into the EU, each production batch must undergo in a Member State a full qualitative analysis, a quantitative analysis of at least the active substances, and all other tests or checks necessary to ensure the quality of medicinal products in accordance with the requirements of the marketing authorization.
rules.source_section: Section 5.3 - Retesting upon importation from third country
rules.verbatim_quote: For products coming from third countries into the EU it is a requirement in Directive 2001/83/EC "that each production batch has undergone in a Member State a full qualitative analysis, a quantitative analysis of at least the active substances and all the other tests or checks necessary to ensure the quality of medicinal products in accordance with the requirements of the marketing authorization".
rules.exceptions: When a company has approval for RTRT for one or more tests in the specification, those tests would not be considered a 'necessary test or check' and a relief from this testing will be accepted.

rules.rule_text: Identification upon receipt of material as part of GMP will apply even if the test is subject to RTRT.
rules.source_section: Section 5.3 - Retesting upon importation from third country
rules.verbatim_quote: Identification upon receipt of material as part of GMP will apply even if this test subject to RTRT.

rules.rule_text: The authorization of the RTRT programme will be granted for specified sites on the basis of an assessment of the product and process understanding together with the proposed control strategy as described in the submission and implemented in accordance with GMP.
rules.source_section: Section 6.1 - General requirements
rules.verbatim_quote: The authorization of the RTRT programme will be granted for specified sites on the basis of an assessment of the product and process understanding together with the proposed control strategy as described in the submission and implemented in accordance with GMP.

rules.rule_text: Monitoring of critical parameters/attributes must be capable of demonstrating that pre-determined validated conditions have been achieved throughout the batch.
rules.source_section: Section 6.1 - General requirements
rules.verbatim_quote: Monitoring of critical parameters/attributes must be capable of demonstrating that pre-determined validated conditions have been achieved throughout the batch.

rules.rule_text: The approval of RTRT by the competent authority will be based on an assessment of the regulatory submission as well as the knowledge of sufficient experience with the process and the GMP compliance at the actual site.
rules.source_section: Section 6.1 - General requirements
rules.verbatim_quote: The approval of RTRT by the competent authority will be based on an assessment of the regulatory submission as well as the knowledge of sufficient experience with the process and the GMP compliance at the actual site.

rules.rule_text: The application for RTRT authorization must demonstrate: that pharmaceutical development studies have identified the critical quality attributes; that a risk-based development program has been carried out; that a scientifically based control strategy has been developed and implemented; that the manufacturing process is or will be validated adequately; that in-process requirements chosen for approval/rejection are decided on the basis of acceptance criteria defined in development studies; the relationship between end-product testing and RTRT including justification of acceptance criteria; that clear procedures are in place for reporting and actions on approval/rejection; that the applied technologies give acceptable quality; comparative test results supporting the relationship between end-product specification and RTRT; that the RTRT approach is equivalent to or better than the end product test; and that a contingency plan is in place.
rules.source_section: Section 6.2 - Documentation
rules.verbatim_quote: The application upon which an authorization may be granted should demonstrate (not unique to a RTRT application): that the pharmaceutical development studies have identified the critical quality attributes for the finished product; that a risk based development program has been carried out; that a scientifically based control strategy has been developed and implemented; that the manufacturing process is, or will be, validated adequately (as evaluated on inspection); that in process requirements chosen for approval/rejection are decided on the basis of the acceptance criteria defined in the development studies; the relationship between end-product testing and RTRT, including justification of acceptance criteria; that clear, specified procedures are in place describing the reporting and actions to be taken on approval/rejection; that the applied technologies gives an acceptable quality; comparative test results (parallel testing) supporting the relationship between the end-product specification and the RTRT where applicable; that the RTRT approach is equivalent to or better than the end product test; that a contingency plan as described in 5.1 is in place.
rules.exceptions: Some elements are noted as 'not unique to a RTRT application'.

rules.rule_text: Parametric release can only be applied to products sterilised in their final containers by moist heat, dry heat or radiation; the sterilisation process in an application for parametric release of sterility must be in accordance with the requirements of the European Pharmacopoeia.
rules.source_section: Section 7 - Parametric Release and Sterilisation
rules.verbatim_quote: The sterilisation process in an application for parametric release of sterility must be in accordance with the requirements of the European Pharmacopoeia. Consequently, parametric release can only be applied to products sterilised in their final containers by moist heat, dry heat or radiation.
cross_references: Ph Eur 6th Ed, 5.1.1 Methods of preparation of sterile products

rules.rule_text: The choice of a sterilisation process must be well founded considering both the knowledge of the stability of the product under relevant conditions and the data gained in development studies where critical process parameters are identified.
rules.source_section: Section 7 - Parametric Release and Sterilisation
rules.verbatim_quote: The choice of a sterilisation process must be well founded considering both the knowledge of the stability of the product under relevant conditions and the data gained in development studies where critical process parameters are identified.

rules.rule_text: When parametric release has been approved it must be routinely used for batch release; in the event that the results fail or are trending toward failure, parametric release may not be substituted by end-product sterility testing, and any failure must be investigated with trending followed up appropriately.
rules.source_section: Section 7 - Parametric Release and Sterilisation
rules.verbatim_quote: When parametric release has been approved this should be routinely used for batch release. In the event that the results fail or are trending toward failure, parametric release may not be substituted by end-product sterility testing. Any failure should be investigated and trending should be followed up appropriately.

rules.rule_text: A sterilisation process must be validated in accordance with GMP guidelines, including heat distribution and heat penetration studies with a given established load pattern so that heat equivalents can be calculated.
rules.source_section: Section 7.1 - Sterilisation by moist and dry heat
rules.verbatim_quote: A sterilisation process shall be validated in accordance with GMP guidelines. Qualification of equipment and validation of the process which is applied at a particular time, including heat distribution and heat penetration studies with a given, established load pattern are thus carried out so that heat equivalents can be calculated.

rules.rule_text: The technical validation of a heat sterilisation method must be complemented by microbiological performance qualification, with consideration given to the level and heat resistance of the microorganisms associated with the product.
rules.source_section: Section 7.1 - Sterilisation by moist and dry heat
rules.verbatim_quote: The technical validation of a heat sterilisation method should be complemented by microbiological performance qualification. Consideration should be given to the level and heat resistance of the microorganisms associated with the product.

rules.rule_text: When the sterilisation process has been defined, its reproducibility shall be demonstrated and compliance with specific GMP requirements as described in Annex 17 to the EU-GMP must also be demonstrated.
rules.source_section: Section 7.1 - Sterilisation by moist and dry heat
rules.verbatim_quote: When the sterilisation process has been defined, its reproducibility shall be demonstrated. Compliance with specific GMP requirements as described in the annex 17 to the EU-GMP should also be demonstrated.

rules.rule_text: An application for parametric release of sterility must be supported by: a description of the sterilisation process including type of cycle, load pattern, specifications for cycle parameters and chemical indicators; specifications and methods for in-process controls including pre-sterilisation bioburden monitoring and verification of load sterilisation; a process validation report comprising heat distribution and heat penetration studies for at least three runs for each load pattern used with microbiological performance qualification; and package integrity data if applicable.
rules.source_section: Section 7.1 - Sterilisation by moist and dry heat
rules.verbatim_quote: An application for parametric release of sterility should be supported by a description of the sterilisation process including type of cycle, load pattern, specifications for cycle parameters (time, temperature, pressure, F0/FH-value) and chemical indicators (if applicable); specifications and methods/procedures used for in-process controls e.g. pre-sterilisation bioburden, monitoring of cycle parameters and verification of load sterilisation; a process validation report comprising heat distribution and heat penetration studies for at least three runs for each load pattern used, and a microbiological performance qualification showing sufficient efficacy (SAL) at the minimum level of the cycle including information on the biological indicators used (type, D-value, Z-value, stability), and bioburden characteristics (number, type, resistance) as applicable; package integrity data (if applicable).
rules.exceptions: Package integrity data required only if applicable.

rules.rule_text: The risk assessment presented in the application for parametric release must focus on the failure to achieve sterility in each unit of every batch, and must include: consistency of performance of the sterilisation process within validated limits; experience with the product applied for and similar products; risks associated with any changes made to the product, process or equipment since the first approval; and steps taken to assess and control identified risks.
rules.source_section: Section 7.1 - Sterilisation by moist and dry heat
rules.verbatim_quote: It is suggested that the risk assessment presented in the application focus on the failure to achieve sterility in each unit of every batch. The risk assessment should include: consistency of performance of the sterilisation process within validated limits; experience with the product applied for and similar products; risks associated with any changes made to the product, or process or equipment since the first approval; steps taken to assess and control identified risks.

rules.rule_text: For sterilisation by radiation, the minimum absorbed dose must generally be 25 kGy; lower doses may be acceptable if justified by routinely collected bioburden data comprising both count and type of microorganisms as well as adequate validation data.
rules.source_section: Section 7.2 - Sterilisation by radiation
rules.verbatim_quote: The minimum absorbed dose should generally be 25 kGy. Lower doses can be acceptable if justified by routinely collected bioburden data comprising both count and type of microorganisms as well as adequate validation data.
rules.exceptions: Lower doses acceptable if justified by routinely collected bioburden data and adequate validation data.
cross_references: NfG on The use of Ionisation Radiation in the Manufacture of Medicinal products (III/9109/90)

rules.rule_text: For sterilisation by radiation, the same documentation requirements as for sterilisation by heat must be met where applicable, and the documentation shall comply with the guidelines defined by the EU in regard to ionising radiation.
rules.source_section: Section 7.2 - Sterilisation by radiation
rules.verbatim_quote: The same requirements regarding documentation as for sterilisation by heat must be met where applicable. The documentation shall comply with the guidelines defined by the EU in regard to ionising radiation.
cross_references: NfG on The use of Ionisation Radiation in the Manufacture of Medicinal products (III/9109/90)

rules.rule_text: This guideline should be read in conjunction with the introduction and general principles and part I of the Annex I to Directive 2001/83/EC as amended.
rules.source_section: Section 3 - Legal basis
rules.verbatim_quote: This guideline should be read in conjunction with the introduction and general principles (4) and part I of the Annex I to Directive 2001/83/83 as amended.
